texto: Sorrow is God's plowshare that turns up and subsoils the depths of the soul, that it may yield richer harvests. If we had never fallen, or were in a glorified state, then the strong torrents of Divine joy would be the normal force to open up all our souls' capacities; but in a fallen world, sorrow, with despair taken out of it, is the chosen power to reveal ourselves to ourselves. Hence it is sorrow that makes us think deeply, long, and soberly.
explicacao: Citação de Lettie B. Cowman sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Sorrow is God's plowshare that turns up and subsoils the depths of the soul, that it may yield ri…” articula reflexao, brokenness, despair em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como despair e sorrow.
